2606.13254.title: Evaluating Pluralism in LLMs through Latent Perspectives
2606.13254.paper_summary: The growing need to represent diverse perspectives has increased interest in pluralistic LLM generation. Although difficult to operationalize, identifying perspectives expressed in text would provide clear guidance on pluralistic alignment and more clearly articulate the pluralistic gap in LLM generation. While models have been shown to reduce the diversity of training data and generate homogeneously, this has been demonstrated primarily on multiple-choice questionnaires or using high-level characteristics of free-form text. In this paper, we introduce and implement a domain-agnostic multi-layered framework for unsupervised extraction of perspectives suitable for identifying the pluralistic gap in LLM-generated text. We evaluate our framework on book reviews, a highly opinionated dataset representing diverse perspectives, and compare various prompts and models. Our results show that while some models and prompting techniques come close to covering a broad spectrum of perspectives, rarer perspectives remain disproportionately underrepresented, resulting in distributions that diverge from human text.
2606.13254.paper_summary_zh: 日益增長的需求來代表多元的觀點，使得對於多元化LLM生成的興趣增加。雖然難以操作化，但識別文本中表達的觀點將為多元對齊提供明確的指導，並更清楚地闡明LLM生成中的多元差距。儘管模型已被證明會減少訓練數據的多樣性並生成同質化的內容，但這主要是在多選問卷或使用自由形式文本的高級特徵上進行的。在本文中，我們介紹並實施了一個領域無關的多層框架，用於無監督地提取適合識別LLM生成文本中的多元差距的觀點。我們在書評上評估我們的框架，這是一個高度意見化的數據集，代表了多元的觀點，並比較了各種提示和模型。我們的結果顯示，儘管一些模型和提示技術接近涵蓋廣泛的觀點，但較少見的觀點仍然不成比例地被低估，導致分佈與人類文本有所偏離。
2606.13254.author: Laura Majer et.al.
2606.13254.authors: Laura Majer, Jan Šnajder, Martin Tutek
